Enemies have 1000% more health, but this buff is decreased by 100% for each Control Champion you have in your formation, stacking additively. Getting to know Idle Champions: Some Champions with the Control role can push enemies away from the formation, protecting your front line.

variants.name: The Champions That Crit
variants.restrictionsText:
Catti-brie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
Champions have an additional 20% critical hit chance.
Champions' normal attacks only deal damage when they are Critical Hits.
Two local residents join the formation to experience life as  adventurers. They're a little battle-shy and don't attack or assist in any way.
Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 50.

variants.name: The Fodder That Fights
variants.restrictionsText:
Catti-brie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use unaffiliated Champions and/or Companions of the Hall Champions.
Each unaffiliated Champion in the formation adds 1 Mithral Hall stack.
Every 75 areas, up to two random Champions are removed from the formation. They can't be used for the rest of the adventure. Champions from the Companions of the Hall affiliation are unaffected by this.
Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 75.

variants.name: An Arranged Welcome
variants.restrictionsText: Melf starts in the formation. He can't be moved or removed. You may only use Champions that have positional formation abilities. You may not place Champions with the DPS role next to Melf. Getting to know Idle Champions: A positional formation ability is an ability that applies to only some Champions in specific slots in a formation.

variants.name: A Mixed Welcome
variants.restrictionsText:
Melf starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
Only one Champion of each species can be in the formation at any time. Multispecies Champions count for all of their species. You may use a second elf in addition to Melf.
Quest requirements are doubled on all non-boss areas.

variants.name: An Acidic Welcome
variants.restrictionsText:
Melf starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use one Champion with the DPS role.
If you add a male DPS Champion to the formation, you may not use any other male Champions (other than Melf.)
If you add a female DPS to the formation, you may not use any other female Champions.
A nonbinary DPS allows anyone to be used, and a nonbinary support Champion may always be used.
Acid Bubbles spawn in all non-boss areas. Some bubbles contain additional enemies that must be defeated afterwards. When a bubble is popped (killed), all Champions in the formation take 5% of their maximum health as acid damage. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress.

variants.name: For Vengeance
variants.restrictionsText: Lae'zel starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. After area 50, Champions only deal 1 point of damage with normal attacks that aren't Critical Hits. A lesser Gibbering Mouther spawns with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Some Champions, like Lae'zel, Strongheart, and Catti-brie, have an increased chance to deal high-damage Critical Hits with their attacks.

variants.name: For Resolve
variants.restrictionsText:
Lae'zel starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with a Wisdom of 14 or higher, and/or Dexterity of 16 or higher. You may also use Commodore Krux.
A Githyanki and Githzerai join the formation. Champions between them deal no damage due to their bickering.
An Intellect Devourer spawns with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress.

variants.name: For Victory
variants.restrictionsText:
Lae'zel starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Evil and/or Neutral Champions. You may also use Commodore Krux. 
After area 50, Champions only deal 1 point of damage with normal attacks that aren't Critical Hits.
Enemy speed and damage is increased by 300%.
An armored Mind Flayer Guard spawns with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress.

variants.name: A Good Tank Is Hard To Find
variants.restrictionsText: Dynaheir starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. Other than Krydle, you may not use Tanking Champions. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Tanking Champions keep your formation safe when things get dangerous. Use Dynaheir's specialization choice wisely if you need help finding tanks!

variants.name: Threading the Needle
variants.restrictionsText:
Dynaheir starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with a Constitution of exactly 14, not counting any Circle Magic shenanigans.

variants.name: Nom Nom Nom
variants.restrictionsText: Dynaheir starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. The Devourer joins the formation. After area 50, the Devourer eats the first quest item dropped by each defeated enemy. Getting to Know Idle Champions: The Devourer is consuming the first Quest Item that each enemy drops. If only there were ways to increase that number?

variants.name: An Event Worth Remembering
variants.restrictionsText: Strongheart starts in the formation with his Justice Needs Champions ability unlocked. He can't be moved or removed. You may only use Event Champions. You can get more Event Champions in events, from time gates, or in this campaign! Only Strongheart and Champions affected by his Justice Needs Champions ability can deal damage. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Events are the easiest way to collect and power up your Champions, and Strongheart's scavenger ability gets you additional Event tokens for use in these events!

variants.name: The Critical Hits Strike Back
variants.restrictionsText:
Strongheart starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Good Champions.
After area 50, Champions deal 1 damage with normal attacks until any Champion lands a Critical Hit in the current area.
When a Champion deals a Critical Hit, that Champion takes damage equal to 20% of their maximum health.
Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 50.

variants.name: An Affiliation Affair
variants.restrictionsText:
Strongheart starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
Only Strongheart and Champions from the affiliation(s) with the most members in the formation can deal damage.
Once the formation has 3 Champions from the same affiliation, you can't add any more Champions from that affiliation.

variants.name: Double Standards
variants.restrictionsText: Havilar joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Champions that are a Standard Species. Standard Species are Aasimar, Dragonborn, Dwarf, Elf, Gnome, Goliath, Halfling, Human, Orc, Tiefling, Half-Orc, and Half-Elf Quest requirements are doubled. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Some Speed Champions, like Havilar, increase the rate at which you progress in area quests. Use her and other Speed Champions to get through these increased requirements.

variants.name: Chipping Away
variants.restrictionsText:
Havilar joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with an ability score total of 81 or more.
All enemies have 4 armored health if they don't already have segmented health.
Enemy speed and damage are increased by 100%.
All Champions' base attack cooldowns are increased by 3 seconds.

variants.name: Charging Up
variants.restrictionsText:
Havilar joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Neutral Good, Chaotic Good, and/or Chaotic Neutral Champs.
Each enemy's damage increases by 100% for each second they are active before they attack, stacking multiplicatively.

variants.name: How Low Can You Go?
variants.restrictionsText: Hank joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Champions with an ability score total of 78 or less. Once you have two Champions in the formation with a particular ability score total, you may not add any more Champions with that particular ability score total. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Many variants restrict Champions based on their ability scores. By collecting a bunch of Champions, you'll have more choices in these restrictive variants.

variants.name: One Hit Wonders
variants.restrictionsText:
Hank joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions that are 30 years old or younger. Additionally, you may use Rosie and/or Gromma.
All enemies in a wave spawn at the same time.
Enemies with melee attacks automatically defeat Champions they deal damage to.

variants.name: Crossover Episode
variants.restrictionsText:
Hank joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Good Champions.
Assorted friends join the formation.
At the start of each Boss area, Venger arrives on his nightmare as an additional boss that must be defeated.

variants.name: Uneclipsed
variants.restrictionsText: Kas joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. You may not use any other Champions that have the DPS role. One of Kas's soldiers joins the formation. Only Evil Champions next to the soldier can deal damage with their base attacks. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Champions with the DPS role are your main damage dealers. Choose and position your Champions well to make the most out of your party's main damage dealer!

variants.name: Who's the Boss?
variants.restrictionsText:
Kas joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
Boss health, damage, and speed are increased by 100%.
A boss enemy spawns at the start of the first wave of each non-boss area. These extra bosses do not drop gold or loot bags, or count toward quest progress
Two of Kas's soldiers join the formation. Only Evil Champions next to either soldier can deal damage with their base attacks.

variants.name: No Rest for the Wicked
variants.restrictionsText:
Kas joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area. 
Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health.
At the start of each area, Champions take damage equal to 30% of their max health.
Three of Kas's soldiers join the formation. Only Evil Champions next to either soldier can deal damage with their base attacks.

variants.name: Gone Missing
variants.restrictionsText: Nordom joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. Click damage only deals damage for the first 25 areas. Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure. Once a Champion is placed in the formation they can not be swapped or removed Every 50 areas, 20% of the Champions are removed and locked for the remainder of the adventure. Nordom is immune to this effect. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Once you've unlocked at least two Champions in each bench slot, you can attempt to unlock a second party and run two sets of Champions at the same time! This variant gives you a little practice on how to Split the Party.

variants.name: Clockwork Clash
variants.restrictionsText:
Nordom joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
Only one Champion of each species can be included in the formation at any time. Champions with multiple species count for all of their species.
Non-boss Construct enemies without segmented health have 4 segmented health instead of their normal health
1-2 Modrons spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress.

variants.name: All Roads Lead to R04M
variants.restrictionsText:
Nordom joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with a total ability score of 82 or higher.
R04M starts in the formation. Champions next to R04M have a +2 bonus to all ability scores, but it's too late to help with the variant restriction.

variants.name: Rise of a Legacy
variants.restrictionsText: Duke Ravengard starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Champions with a Melee base attack. After you complete area 50 for the first time, you can't remove Champions from the formation. When you complete this variant, any Champions in the formation will be unavailable in the Burden of Legacy or End of a Legacy variants. Each Patron variant has its own list of unavailable Champions. Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 100. Getting to Know Idle Champions: A Champion's attack is categorized as a Melee, Ranged, and/or a Magic attack. Some Variants and Champion abilities require a Champion to have a specific type of attack!

variants.name: Burden of Legacy
variants.restrictionsText: Duke Ravengard starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Champions with a Melee base attack. You also can't use any Champions that you used to finish variant 1 other than Duke Ravengard. After area 10, all enemies spawn with an extra 10 armored hit points which must be destroyed before their regular hit points appear. When an enemy is knocked back, these armored hit points are immediately removed, revealing its normal health bar. After you complete area 50 for the first time, you can't remove Champions from the formation. When you complete this variant, any Champions in the formation will be unavailable in the End of a Legacy variant. Each Patron variant has its own list of unavailable Champions. Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 100.

variants.name: End of a Legacy
variants.restrictionsText:
Duke Ravengard starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with a Melee base attack. You also can't use any Champions that you used to finish variant 1 and 2 other than Duke Ravengard.
After area 10, enemies spawn with a special shield which must be destroyed before their regular hit points appear. To destroy a special shield, an enemy must be hit with a critical hit.
Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 100.

variants.name: Kobold Without a Cause
variants.restrictionsText: Spurt starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. Only Champions that aren't part of an affiliation can be used. Getting to Know Idle Champions: An Affiliation is a small group of Champions that often have mechanics that encourage using them together. Unaffiliated Champions often have flexible abilities that can be used in many different formations.

variants.name: The Fast and the Flattened
variants.restrictionsText:
Spurt starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions that have a base attack cooldown of 4 seconds or less.
All Enemies move 100% faster and their base attack cooldowns are reduced by 50%.

variants.name: Wasps and the Kobold that Loves Them
variants.restrictionsText:
Spurt starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with both a Dexterity and Charisma of 13 or higher.
Two Hellwasps join the formation. They just get in the way.

variants.name: Power Word Pain
variants.restrictionsText: Baeloth joins the formation. He can't be moved or removed. Only Baeloth and the Champions in the columns ahead and behind him can deal damage with their base attacks. Each time a Champion other than Baeloth deals damage with their base attack, they take damage equal to 5% of their max health. Each time a Champion other than Baeloth uses their ultimate, they take damage equal to 50% of their max health. Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area. Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Some Champions like Baeloth can prevent a Champion from dying. This often provides your formation with the final push you need to succeed!

variants.name: Power Word Stun
variants.restrictionsText:
Baeloth joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with the Gold Find, Hunter, or DPS role.
After area 50, one Champion is stunned in each area. The Champion in seat 1 is stunned first, advancing by bench order in each area. Baeloth's seat is skipped, and this resets after seat 12.
Champions in stunned seats cannot be moved or removed from the formation.

variants.name: Power Word Kill
variants.restrictionsText:
Baeloth joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
After area 50, a random Champion other than Baeloth takes damage equal to 100% of their max health every 10 seconds.

variants.name: Three Wolf Moon
variants.restrictionsText: Shadowheart joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. In non-boss areas, 3 Wolves spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. These Wolves are armored enemies that move 200% faster and deal 400% more damage. Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area. Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Champions with the Healing role let other heroes recover health or gain temporary health that can go above normal maximums.

variants.name: Waxing or Waning
variants.restrictionsText:
Shadowheart joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with a Dexterity of 14 or higher.
If the formation includes an Evil Champion, you may not add Good Champions.
If the formation includes a Good Champion, you may not add Evil Champions.
Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area.
Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health.

variants.name: Can't Cast a Shadow Without Some Light
variants.restrictionsText:
Shadowheart joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
Other than Shadowheart, you may only use Champions with a Dexterity of 13 or lower.
A Torch joins the formation and bathes all adjacent formation slots in bright Light. Formation slots not adjacent to the torch are cast into Shadow.
Champions in the Light take 5% of their max health as damage every second and can not deal damage with their base attacks.
Champions in the Shadow have their damage boosted by 1000% for each Champion that is in the Light, stacking multiplicatively.
Whenever Shadowheart changes her position in the formation, the torch occupies her previous position (and if she took the spot of another Champion, they are moved to where the torch was).
Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area. 
Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health.
